_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to identify new and emerging vulnerabilities.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (prompt injection, insecure output handling, training data poisoning, model theft, excessive agency).
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.541), SP-S-008 (SP, cosine: 0.534).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.9/10 (raw 0.491). Shared vocabulary: [vulnerabilities]. DCWF-only terms [ability, emerging, identify, new] vs WIDAI-only terms [10, agency, applications, data, excessive, handling] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.863 (P=0.899, R=0.829). P exceeds R by 0.070 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Ability to identify new and emerging vulnerabilities." and "Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 f" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.581 > STS 0.491 (gap +0.090). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [vulnerabilities] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.2%. Shared tokens: [vulnerabilities]. DCWF-only: [ability, emerging, identify, new]. WIDAI-only: [10, agency, applications, data, excessive, handling].

signals.detail: STS 0.491 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
